description:
You are acting as a junior log analyst.  
Inside the workstation you will find a CSV-formatted log called  
/home/user/logs/system_events.csv

1. Preview that file to understand its structure.  
   • It is comma-separated.  
   • The columns (in order) are: timestamp,event,user,code  
   • There is a single header line followed by five data lines.

2. Using only a small chain of classic text-processing tools (a total of two or three commands is enough), do the following column manipulation work:  
   a. Cut out the 1st column (timestamp) into one temporary stream or file.  
   b. Cut out the 3rd column (user) into a second temporary stream or file.  
   c. Paste those two cuts side-by-side so that timestamp is the first column and user is the second column, separated by a single TAB character.  
   d. Save the final result as /home/user/logs/event_report.tsv

3. Make sure the resulting file /home/user/logs/event_report.tsv satisfies ALL of the following format requirements (the automated grader is case- and byte-exact):  
   • It must contain exactly six lines: one header line plus five data lines.  
   • Column separator: exactly one horizontal TAB character (ASCII 0x09).  
   • The header line must read:  
     timestamp<TAB>user  
   • Each data line must retain the original timestamp and user values, preserving their order of appearance in system_events.csv.  
   • No extra spaces, no additional columns, and the file must end with a single trailing newline.

Once the file is created exactly as described, the task is complete.  
Hint: a standard solution can be done with just “cut …”, “cut …”, and “paste …”.
truth:
Initial files/directories already present before the agent starts:

1. Directory: /home/user/logs   (permissions 755)
2. File: /home/user/logs/system_events.csv   (permissions 644)
   Exact byte content (each “\n” represents a UNIX newline):
timestamp,event,user,code\n
2023-03-10 12:01:05,LOGIN_SUCCESS,alice,200\n
2023-03-10 12:05:00,LOGIN_FAILURE,bob,403\n
2023-03-10 12:10:15,FILE_UPLOAD,charlie,201\n
2023-03-10 12:12:47,LOGIN_SUCCESS,dave,200\n
2023-03-10 12:15:30,FILE_DELETE,alice,204\n

No other files are initially in /home/user/logs.

File that MUST exist after the agent finishes:

/home/user/logs/event_report.tsv   (permissions can be 644).  
Exact required byte content (each “\t” is a TAB, “\n” is a UNIX newline):

timestamp\tuser\n
2023-03-10 12:01:05\talice\n
2023-03-10 12:05:00\tbob\n
2023-03-10 12:10:15\tcharlie\n
2023-03-10 12:12:47\tdave\n
2023-03-10 12:15:30\talice\n

The grader will:

1. Verify that /home/user/logs/event_report.tsv exists.  
2. Confirm it contains exactly 6 lines.  
3. Confirm the bytes match the content block above exactly (no trailing spaces, tabs, or extra newlines).  
4. Optionally, check that the agent did not modify /home/user/logs/system_events.csv.  

Any temporary files the agent creates inside /home/user/logs are allowed but are not required for grading.
name: task_000000_d3f56880
